name: Serbia
stopoverNote: Belgrade works well as a 2–3 night city break or a longer Serbia loop with Novi Sad, Niš and Zlatibor.

cities.name: Belgrade
cities.notes: Big-city base for Kalemegdan, Saint Sava Temple, riverfront neighborhoods and Serbia's best museum mix

cities.name: Novi Sad
cities.notes: Relaxed Danube city with Petrovaradin Fortress, museums and easy day-trip potential toward Fruška Gora

cities.name: Niš
cities.notes: Southern Serbia stop for Roman ruins, Ottoman walls and sobering WWII and uprising-era memorials

cities.name: Zlatibor
cities.notes: Mountain resort base for gondola rides, village museums and cave stops in western Serbia

cities.name: Subotica
cities.notes: Elegant northern city known for Hungarian Secession architecture, the synagogue and Palić side trips

itinerary.sim: Yettel tourist SIMs usually land around ₹770 for 30 days with 50 GB; mts Tourist Max is about ₹460 for 14 days with 10 GB if you want the cheaper walk-in option.

Belgrade.name: Belgrade
Belgrade.note: Two full days cover the essentials; keep a third if you want a slower Zemun afternoon or a museum-heavy Sunday when the National Museum is free.

days.theme: Kalemegdan views, republic-square museums and old-town energy

activities.name: Belgrade Fortress and Kalemegdan Park walk
National Museum of Serbia combined ticket
Knez Mihailova pedestrian street and Republic Square wander
Skadarlija evening stroll

days.theme: Tesla exhibits, Saint Sava interiors and Yugoslav history

activities.name: Nikola Tesla Museum English guided tour
activities.tip: Carry cash in dinars because museum payment policies can change faster than card terminals.

activities.name: Saint Sava Temple visit
Museum of Yugoslavia and House of Flowers
Zemun quay and Gardoš area sunset walk

Novi Sad.name: Novi Sad
Novi Sad.note: A single day works well on the high-speed rail from Belgrade, but add a second if you want a fortress tunnel tour plus a slower Danube evening or Fruška Gora detour.

days.theme: Soko rail hop, Petrovaradin ramparts and Vojvodina art

activities.name: Srbija Voz Soko train from Belgrade to Novi Sad
Petrovaradin Fortress ramparts and viewpoint walk
Novi Sad City Museum underground-galleries tour
Gallery of Matica Srpska

Subotica.name: Subotica
Subotica.note: Subotica is compact enough for a focused old-town day, especially if you stay central and book the midday City Hall tour.

days.theme: Regional train north, Art Nouveau landmarks and synagogue interiors

activities.name: Srbija Voz train from Novi Sad to Subotica
Subotica Synagogue entry
Subotica City Hall and tower guided tour
Raichle Palace / Modern Art Gallery

Niš.name: Niš
Niš.note: Niš can be seen quickly, but the city lands better with an extra night if you want Mediana, the Red Cross memorial and an unhurried dinner in Tinkers Alley.

days.theme: Cross-country rail day, Roman ruins and Serbia's hardest history stop

activities.name: Srbija Voz connection from Subotica to Niš via Novi Sad and Belgrade
Archaeological Park Mediana
Skull Tower (Ćele Kula)
Red Cross Nazi Concentration Camp memorial museum
Niš Fortress evening walk

Zlatibor.name: Zlatibor
Zlatibor.note: Use Zlatibor as the nature reset at the end of the route; two nights are ideal because the Gold Gondola, cave and village museum are weather-dependent and spread out.

days.theme: Niš Ekspres bus, lakefront strolls and the long Gold Gondola ride

activities.name: Niš Ekspres or Lasta bus from Niš to Zlatibor
Gold Gondola Zlatibor return ride
Lake Zlatibor promenade and central park walk
Dino Park Zlatibor

days.theme: Karst cave stop, old-village museum and a slower mountain day

activities.name: Stopića Cave
Sirogojno Open-Air Museum
Tornik viewpoint or meadow walk
Zlatibor market and dairy-shopping stop

connections.method: Direct Srbija Voz Soko high-speed train in about 36–55 minutes
Regional Srbija Voz train in about 1h 50m to 2h 20m
Srbija Voz rail connection via Novi Sad and Belgrade in about 6h 30m to 7h 30m
Niš Ekspres or Lasta bus in about 4h 30m to 6h
Lasta or other direct intercity bus in about 4h to 4h 30m

extras: National Museum of Serbia is usually free on Sundays, so line up Belgrade museum time there if your dates fit.
Keep ₹1,500–₹2,500 equivalent in cash for museum kiosks, buses and smaller kafanas because card acceptance is less reliable once you leave central Belgrade and Novi Sad.
Book the Belgrade–Novi Sad Soko train ahead on weekends and around EXIT Festival because the fastest departures can sell out.
December works best for Belgrade and Subotica Christmas-market atmosphere, but mountain weather can interrupt the Gold Gondola, so keep Zlatibor flexible.
If you only have 5–6 days, cut Subotica first; if you have more than a week, add a second Niš or Novi Sad night instead of overpacking Belgrade.
